free_text: Measurement and analysis of the excitation function
and isomeric cross section ratios for alpha-induced
reaction on Ir-, Au-, Re- and Ta-nuclei
The stopping
powers from the tabulated values.
derived fusion
cross sections given
The targets were thin self-supporting foils. The
thicknesses of gold foils were 4.837-mu-m, iridium
foils 13.021-mu-m and tantalum foils were 10.521-mu-m
obtained by weighing. The Au- and Ta-stack consisted
of about 20 targets whereas Ir-stack consisted of about
15 targets. Since the Au-foils were very thin, these
interspersed with 23.4-mu-m thick Al-degrader-cum-
catcher foils.
-Equilibrium and pre-equilibrium decays. STAPRE
and ALICE-91 codes.
The 224-cm variable energy cyclotron.
Un-analyzed external beam.
The  absolute errors which is generally ~8% consists of
uncertainties:
Due to target thickness,
Due to the beam current integration,
Due to the detector efficiency,
The  uncertainties caused by the large size of
the irradiation area and the non-uniformities of the
target.
Only statistical uncertainties are given in
data tables.
The uncertainty given for the energy values
are those of target thickness only and beam energy
resolution was ~ +-200-keV.
Correction
Remark of N.Otsuka is included.
Last checking has been done.
SD: SF5=(M) added to REACTION code
in Subent 014
SD: Ref. PRM,51,743,1998 to REL-REF added.
Small corrections in Subents 002-016. ERR-ANALYS update
DECAY-DATA corrected in several Subents.
SD:Zeros were added to right side of values
following presentation in the Table. STATUS added.
DECAY-DATA update. SF5=M+ deleted from REACTION code.
.Since the radio-nuclide Au-193 could be produced
by Ir-191(alpha,2n) and Ir-193(alpha,4n) reactions in
the overlapping regions the cross section for these
reactions are taken as proportional to their
theoretical values based on the GDH-model calculation.
